title: 1D Batch Processing and SAND Using NMRPipe
author: Christopher Esselman
materials: Sign in to your NMRbox account.

steps.title: Open terminal
steps.instruction: Open the terminal, type csh, and press Enter.

steps.title: Retrieve sand_demo1 files
steps.instruction: Paste the command provided below into the terminal window and press Enter. Retrieve sand_demo1 files wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/edisonomics/FLAnalyzer/main/sand/sand_demo1.tar

steps.title: Extract sand_demo1.tar
steps.instruction: Open the File Manager and extract sand_demo1.tar to the home directory by right-clicking on sand_demo1.tar and selecting "Extract Here."

steps.title: Copy .com files
steps.instruction: Navigate to the extracted sand_demo1 directory, then copy and paste each ".com" file into the directory that contains your unprocessed data.

steps.title: Copy meta.tab
steps.instruction: Create a copy of meta.tab and place it into the directory containing your unprocessed data.

steps.title: Update meta.tab
steps.instruction: Update meta.tab to match the data within your unprocessed directory. Specifically, change the "DIR_NAME" column (the second column of numbers) to match the directories of the spectra you wish to batch process. You should also change the "TITLE" column (the third column) to describe the spectra you are processing, ensuring that there are no spaces in the title name.

steps.title: Navigate to data directory
steps.instruction: Using a new terminal window, navigate (cd) into the directory containing the unprocessed data, ".com" files, and meta.tab.

steps.title: Run batch processing
steps.instruction: Enter the command ./all.com The data processing is complete once a graphical image of the spectra appears on the screen. The final processed data will be located in the newly created folder named interp.

steps.title: View processed spectra
steps.instruction: To view the spectra at any time after processing, open a terminal, navigate to the directory containing the unprocessed and processed data, and enter the command specView.tcl -in interp/*.ft1

steps.title: Place the metaTabCreate
steps.instruction: Place the metaTabCreate.sh script in the same directory as the meta.tab file you just moved.

steps.title: Ensure the file is executable by right-clicking on metaTabCreate
steps.instruction: Ensure the file is executable by right-clicking on metaTabCreate.sh, choosing "Properties", selecting "Permissions," and checking "Allow this file to run as a program".

steps.title: Using a terminal, navigate (cd) to...
steps.instruction: Using a terminal, navigate (cd) to the directory containing the files and enter the command ./metaTabCreate.sh

steps.title: Provide a title for the dataset,...
steps.instruction: Provide a title for the dataset, ensuring you do not use spaces in the name.

steps.title: Verify meta.tab content
steps.instruction: Ensure that the rows in the meta.tab file contain metadata only for the files you intend to process. You should delete any rows corresponding to folders that do not contain data or should not be processed.

steps.title: Troubleshoot phasing
steps.instruction: Sometimes, the automatic zero-order phasing does not phase the spectra perfectly. If this is the case, please follow the steps below.

steps.title: Reprocess with manual phasing
steps.instruction: Visualize the spectra to identify which spectra are not phased correctly. Then, reprocess the chosen spectra with 0.0 zero-order phasing and 0.0 first-order phasing by changing the "P0" values for those spectra in meta.tab from Auto to 0.0 and then reexecuting ./all.com

steps.title: Open nmrDraw
steps.instruction: After processing is complete, open a new terminal, navigate to the interp folder, and enter the command nmrDraw Note that nmrDraw functions best when used with a three-button mouse. Further directions on spectral processing and use of NMRPipe can be found at: https://www.ibbr.umd.edu/nmrpipe/index.html

steps.title: Select spectrum for phasing
steps.instruction: Select the spectrum you want to phase by first right-clicking on "File" and then left-clicking on "Select File".

steps.title: Scroll and select spectrum
steps.instruction: Use the top sliding bar to scroll through the spectra, and double left-click on the specific spectrum you wish to phase.

steps.title: Phase spectra
steps.instruction: Use the sliders to phase the spectra by left-clicking and dragging them.

steps.title: Record phasing values
steps.instruction: After the phasing looks correct, the values shown in the red box below will be the values used to replace the zero-order and first-order phase values for that spectrum in the meta.tab file.

steps.title: Reexecute after phasing
steps.instruction: After you have changed the phasing values in the meta.tab file, reexecute
